sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse buys site in 2534 development
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse has purchased a 2.326-acre site in Johnstown, Colorado for $1.925 million to build an 8,000-square-foot steakhouse. This new restaurant will be located in the 2534 master-planned community, adjacent to a future Boondocks Fun Center, and is expected to bring jobs and dining options to the area. The Town of Johnstown mayor welcomed the development, highlighting Texas Roadhouse's commitment to sustainability and community involvement.

feed.title: Willis Lease Finance Corp CEO Sells Shares Worth Over $666K
feed.summary: Willis Austin Chandler, CEO and 10% Owner of Willis Lease Finance Corp, sold 3,400 shares of common stock for over $666,000 on March 2, 2026. These transactions were executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan at weighted average prices between $194.4479 and $200.89. After the sale, Chandler still holds a significant number of shares directly and indirectly through family trusts and partnerships.

feed.title: Texas Capital Bancshares Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. has seen its stock price revised amidst current market dynamics, with a notable year-to-date return of 5.26% and a one-year return of 21.26%, outperforming the S&P 500. Despite some short-term mixed signals from Dow Theory, technical indicators largely suggest a positive outlook. The company's strong three-year return of 44.57% highlights its potential in the banking sector.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering $2,140,000 in Review Notes linked to the lesser performance of the Russell 2000® Futures Excess Return Index and the iShares® MSCI EAFE ETF, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. fully and unconditionally guaranteeing the payments. These notes are designed for investors seeking an early exit at a premium if specific conditions are met on review dates, but investors risk losing a significant portion or all of their principal at maturity. The notes are complex, unsecured obligations that do not pay interest, and are subject to various market, credit, and liquidity risks.

feed.title: Daqo New Energy Corp. reports FY2025 financial results
feed.summary: Daqo New Energy Corp. announced its FY2025 financial results, showing a decline in revenue to $665.4 million due to lower polysilicon prices and reduced sales volumes. Despite a gross loss, the company improved its EBITDA to $1.7 million from a negative $337.4 million in FY2024. The net loss attributable to shareholders also decreased, and the company projects increased polysilicon production for FY2026.

feed.title: MetLife Inc (MET, US59156R1086): How the Global Life Insurer Positions Investors for a Higher-Rate 2
feed.summary: This article analyzes MetLife Inc. (MET) as a global insurance and retirement investment, especially in a higher-interest-rate environment for 2026. It explores MetLife's business model, global footprint, regulatory landscape, and investment portfolio, highlighting how the company's diversified operations and capital management strategy position it for future growth and investor returns. The piece also discusses key risks and how MetLife fits into global investment portfolios, emphasizing its role for income-seeking and defensive investors.

feed.title: Julie Turpin PurposeFULL Leadership Newsletter - What’s The Vibe?
feed.summary: In her "PurposeFULL Leadership" newsletter, Julie Turpin discusses the importance of workplace culture, especially during Insurance Careers Month. She emphasizes that while speed often overtakes discernment in hiring and team movements, culture significantly impacts job satisfaction and employee retention. Turpin provides a proven culture evaluation process, including self-reflection, seeking consistency, experiencing the physical environment, asking perceptive questions, and trusting intuition, to help individuals find a company whose "vibe" aligns with their values.

feed.title: Campbell Soup (Texas) WARN Act Investigation
feed.summary: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating Campbell Soup Supply Company LLC regarding a potential mass layoff of 205 employees in Paris, Texas, on March 3, 2026. The firm believes Campbell Soup may have violated the federal WARN Act by potentially failing to provide the required 60-day notice to employees before the layoff. Employees impacted by this layoff may be entitled to 60 days of severance pay and benefits, and the law firm is seeking to speak with affected individuals.

feed.title: Is Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (APD) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (APD), highlighting its position as a high-quality industrial gas leader trading at a discount despite a recent stock dip. The market's reaction to talks with Yara International is seen as disconnected from fundamentals, as management reconfirmed its 2026 adjusted EPS guidance. APD features a strong dividend growth streak and an attractive risk-reward profile, with major capital expenditures winding down, forecast to boost free cash flow.

feed.title: Allen Weinberg Appointed Chief Growth and Strategy Officer at Broadridge Financial Solutions
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions has announced the appointment of Allen Weinberg as its new Chief Growth and Strategy Officer. In this role, Weinberg will be responsible for overseeing the company's strategic initiatives, identifying new market opportunities, fostering innovation, and aligning strategic goals within the financial services sector. This appointment underscores Broadridge's commitment to strengthening its leadership team for continuous expansion and adaptation.

feed.title: AI Is Increasing Uncertainty of Long-Term Moats Around Software, Including for Adobe
feed.summary: The article discusses how AI is increasing uncertainty around the long-term competitive advantages (moats) of software companies, specifically mentioning Adobe. It highlights Adobe's established position in content creation with products like Photoshop and Illustrator, and its expansion through Adobe Express and the AI solution Firefly. The author believes Adobe is strategically focusing on attracting new users, which will become increasingly important for the company's future success.

feed.title: Layoffs at Campbell's Soup factory near Oklahoma border top 200
feed.summary: Campbell's will lay off over 200 workers at its Paris, Texas factory. The company plans to change the plant's operational focus from soup production to other products, requiring fewer employees. The Paris factory has been in operation since 1964.

feed.title: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE:FIX) Director Sells $6,163,830.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE:FIX) Director Darcy Anderson sold 4,500 shares of the company's stock, valued at $6,163,830, reducing his ownership by nearly 20%. Following the sale, the stock dropped about 7.2%, though the company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Buy" consensus rating for FIX with an average target price of $1,573.67.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE:FIX) SVP Sells 671 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE:FIX) SVP Terrence Reed recently sold 671 shares of the company's stock on February 24th at an average price of $1,457.17, totaling approximately $977,761, decreasing his stake by 14.52%. He also sold an additional 1,116 shares on March 3rd for about $1.56 million. These transactions occur as the company reports strong financial results, beating Q4 expectations, increasing its dividend, and maintaining a "Buy" consensus rating from analysts with a $1,573.67 price target.

feed.title: Is Waste Management, Inc. (WM) A Good Stock To Buy?
feed.summary: An article by Ricardo Pillai summarizes a bullish thesis on Waste Management, Inc. (WM), the largest environmental services company in North America. The thesis highlights WM's vertically integrated system, significant infrastructure ownership (including 30% of U.S. landfill capacity), and strong financial performance with projected free cash flow of $3.8 billion by 2026. The company's operational efficiency, strategic acquisitions like Stericycle, and investments in renewable energy further support its long-term growth and robust shareholder returns.

feed.title: Ross Stores reports 4Q earnings well above guidance
feed.summary: Ross Stores Inc. announced that its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings significantly surpassed expectations, with a 12% increase in total sales and a 9% rise in comparable-store sales. The off-price retailer's full-year sales reached a record $22.8 billion, driven by strong holiday season performance and improved customer engagement. CEO Jim Conroy noted accelerating business momentum in the latter half of the year, despite earlier macroeconomic challenges, positioning the company well for the future.

feed.title: Oracle: Despite Some AI Benefits, Lowering Moat Rating On Disruption Threat
feed.summary: Morningstar has downgraded Oracle's economic moat rating from wide to narrow and reduced its fair value estimate to $215 per share from $277. This adjustment reflects concerns about AI's potential disruption to Oracle's core database and enterprise software, despite the company's Oracle Cloud Infrastructure being poised to benefit from generative AI. The forecast for Oracle Cloud Applications' five-year annualized growth has also been lowered due to customer hesitation regarding "agentic" AI adoption.

feed.title: Do Apple’s new low-cost devices have too many compromises?
feed.summary: Apple is launching new low-cost devices, the $599 iPhone 17e and $599 MacBook Neo, aiming to broaden its market appeal amidst rising component costs for other tech companies. While the iPhone 17e is expected to maintain Apple's performance standards, the MacBook Neo's use of an A-series chip and limited 8 GB memory raises questions about its performance for more demanding applications. This move by Apple is also spurring competition from PC manufacturers, potentially benefiting consumers looking for affordable notebooks.

feed.title: Evergy files plans for substation to help power $3B data center in De Soto
feed.summary: Evergy has filed plans for a new Blackbird substation in De Soto, Kansas, to support the substantial power demands of a growing area, including a planned $3 billion data center. Construction for the substation is anticipated to commence in the spring. This development is crucial given the recent approval of the large data center project near Panasonic's operations in De Soto.

feed.title: Corning Inc Stock (GLW) Moved Down by 8.62% on Mar 5: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Corning Inc (GLW) stock dropped by 8.62% on March 5, 2026, primarily due to a broader market downturn influenced by geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices. Analyst reports raising valuation concerns and recent insider selling also contributed to the negative sentiment, overshadowing positive company-specific developments like new product launches and strong financial guidance. Technical indicators show neutral to oversold conditions, while fundamental analysis highlights the company's strong revenue and net profit within the Technology Equipment industry, though it faces risks from elevated valuation and capital intensiveness.

feed.title: International Business Machines Corp Stock (IBM) Moved Up by 3.20% on Mar 5: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: International Business Machines Corp (IBM) stock rose by 3.20% on March 5, driven by strategic advancements in AI and quantum computing, including a new Infrastructure Innovation Centre in India and a partnership with Deepgram for the watsonx Orchestrate platform. Positive financial guidance, strong Q4 results, and a "Moderate Buy" analyst consensus further supported the surge. The company's proactive AI developments are outweighing earlier concerns about potential AI disruption to legacy systems.

feed.title: Key facts: EOG Resources expands in UAE and Bahrain; UBS raises price target
feed.summary: EOG Resources Inc. is expanding its international presence by investing in oil and gas in the UAE and Bahrain, securing a 900,000-acre concession and forming a joint venture. Following this, UBS raised its price target for EOG Resources to $158.00 from $149.00, and Piper Sandler also increased its target to $127.00 from $123.00.

feed.title: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) Stock
feed.summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (CIBC) has increased its target price for Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) to C$211, suggesting a potential upside of 7.86%. Despite mixed analyst opinions, with some raising and others lowering targets, the consensus rating remains "Hold" with an average target of C$202.50. BMO shares experienced a slight decline to C$195.62, and the company recently reported C$3.28 earnings per share with C$9.34 billion in revenue for its last quarter.

feed.title: PNC Financial's $2B Branch Expansion Strategy: A Smart Growth Move?
feed.summary: PNC Financial is investing $2 billion to expand its physical branch network, opening over 300 new branches and renovating existing ones by 2029, and hiring 2,000 employees. This strategy aims to drive customer growth and revenue in high-growth markets, leveraging potential loan growth from anticipated interest rate reductions and strengthening customer relationships. Other major banks like Bank of America and JPMorgan are also pursuing significant physical expansion, indicating a growing belief in the importance of physical presence in the banking sector.

feed.title: ZAWYA-PROJECTS: US-based EOG Resources to invest in unconventional oil, gas prospects in UAE and Bahrain
feed.summary: US-based EOG Resources plans to invest in unconventional oil and gas prospects in the UAE and Bahrain as part of its international expansion strategy. The company was awarded an onshore concession in the UAE and entered a joint venture with Bapco in Bahrain in 2025. EOG Resources has earmarked a $6.5 billion capital program for 2026, which includes spending on these international projects.

feed.title: EYEG - AB Corporate Bond ETF Latest Stock News & Market Updates
feed.summary: This page provides the latest news and market updates for AB Corporate Bond ETF (Ticker: EYEG), offering insights for investors and traders. It features high-quality news articles with expert summaries from Rhea-AI, detailing the impact and sentiment of each release on the stock's performance. The platform aims to simplify stock market news by combining real-time updates, AI analysis, and historical stock performance data, catering to both new and experienced traders.

feed.title: Why Is Veralto (VLTO) Up 3.6% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Veralto (VLTO) shares have risen 3.6% since its last earnings report, outperforming the S&P 500. The company reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, with earnings exceeding estimates and solid revenue growth, though revenues were slightly below market expectations. Despite positive performance, current estimates for VLTO are trending downward, leading to a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: MWC 2026 | Fibocom Unveils 5G MiFi Solution Based on Linux OS and Qualcomm QMB415 Platform
feed.summary: Fibocom has launched the FG205 module and a new 5G MiFi solution at MWC 2026, leveraging the Qualcomm QMB415 platform. This solution utilizes a streamlined hardware architecture, reducing memory requirements by supporting the Linux operating system, which in turn lowers costs and minimizes supply risks. The FG205 module also offers pin-to-pin compatibility with previous Fibocom series, allowing for faster product iteration and supporting various Wi-Fi standards.

feed.title: Tourmaline Oil (TSE:TOU) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Raymond James Financial Analyst Says
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has increased its price target for Tourmaline Oil (TSE:TOU) from C$69.00 to C$72.00, suggesting a potential upside of 15.94% from its previous close. This occurs despite recent price target cuts from other firms and a dip in the stock price, with some analysts maintaining "Strong Buy" or "Buy" ratings. Additionally, insider Mike Rose recently purchased 10,000 shares, reflecting confidence in the company, which is Canada's largest and most active natural gas producer.

feed.title: Altria Continues to Lead in US Combustibles, Though It Lags Peers in Reduced-Risk Products
feed.summary: Altria (MO) maintains its dominant position in the U.S. combustible cigarette market, primarily thanks to its Marlboro brand which holds a 40% market share. Despite this, the company faces challenges as the U.S. market relies on price increases to offset volume declines. The article indicates that Altria lags its peers in the reduced-risk product category.

feed.title: Micron Stock Price Prediction: Where Will MU Be in 1 Year?
feed.summary: Micron (MU) saw a significant stock increase after a strong Q1 report, with revenue of $13.64B and EPS of $4.78 beating estimates. The company's sold-out HBM capacity through 2026 and new AI infrastructure products with Nvidia

feed.title: Costco Earnings Due. These Retailers Retreat On Results; One Surges.
feed.summary: Retail stocks saw mixed performance on Thursday following a series of earnings reports. While Costco Wholesale slid ahead of its earnings release and Victoria's Secret and BJ's Wholesale retreated, Burlington surged into a buy zone. FactSet anticipates Costco's fiscal second-quarter earnings to increase by 13% to $4.55 per share.

feed.title: Ford recalls more than 600K vehicles over faulty windshield wipers
feed.summary: Ford is recalling over 600,000 vehicles, including certain 2020-2022 Explorer, Escape, Lincoln Aviator, and Lincoln Corsair models, due to faulty windshield wiper motors that can fail and reduce visibility. The issue stems from a poor connection during assembly. Although Ford initially closed an investigation in 2021, it was reopened in late 2025 after a surge in warranty claims, with repairs offered free of charge to affected owners.

feed.title: J&J wins FDA nod for multiple myeloma therapy (JNJ:NYSE)
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson received FDA approval for its antibody therapy, Tecvayli (teclistamab), to be used in a combination regimen for treating multiple myeloma. The approval specifically allows Tecvayli to be used with Darzalex Faspro (daratumumab). This development is a significant advancement in the treatment options for this type of blood cancer.

feed.title: Citigroup Maintains 'Buy' Rating, Raises Price Target for Darden Restaurants (DRI) | DRI Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has reiterated its 'Buy' rating for Darden Restaurants (DRI) and increased its price target slightly from $234 to $235, as analyzed by Jon Tower. This adjustment comes amidst various recent analyst rating changes for DRI, with some upgrading and others downgrading the stock. Darden Restaurants, the largest full-service restaurant operator in the US, maintains a diverse portfolio of 11 brands, and analysts generally forecast an "Outperform" status for the company, with an average target price of $220.79.

feed.title: Netflix just bought the AI company Ben Affleck apparently founded
feed.summary: Netflix has acquired InterPositive, an AI startup co-founded by Ben Affleck, for an undisclosed amount. Affleck claims the company's AI tools are designed to assist filmmakers by enhancing existing material and protecting human creativity, rather than generating content from scratch. This acquisition comes after Netflix used AI for a scene in "The Eternaut," with Affleck now serving as a senior advisor.

feed.title: Insider Signals: Coca-Cola EVP Nancy Quan Liquidates $1.87 Million in Shares Amid Defensive Sector Peak
feed.summary: Coca-Cola EVP Nancy Quan sold $1.87 million in company stock, part of a larger trend of insider selling as KO shares reached historic highs. This liquidation by top executives coincides with a "flight to safety" into defensive consumer staples, fueled by geopolitical tensions and concerns over tech sector valuations. The sales raise questions about the sustainability of the current rally in consumer staples, with some analysts warning the sector may be overbought.

feed.title: Residential cruise startup acquires 27-year-old ship from for long-term living concept
feed.summary: Avora Residences, a South Florida residential cruise startup, has acquired a 27-year-old ship from Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings. The startup plans to convert the vessel into a floating community designed for long-term living at sea. This acquisition marks a significant step for Avora Residences in establishing its unique long-term residential cruise concept.

feed.title: 10 Monthly Dividend Stocks With Safe Payouts
feed.summary: This article identifies 10 monthly dividend stocks with safe payouts, based on low payout ratios and high Dividend Risk Scores from the Sure Analysis Research Database. The selections, which include REITs, financial institutions, and specialized firms, are presented in order of lowest payout ratio, offering investors potential options for stable, frequent income streams. The piece emphasizes the importance of thorough research due to inherent investment risks.

feed.title: Lennar Is Focusing on Its Core Homebuilding Operation
feed.summary: Lennar, the second-largest homebuilder in the U.S., is concentrating its strategy on its core homebuilding operations. The company constructs single-family homes across 30 states, serving a broad spectrum of buyers from first-time homeowners to luxury clients. This article highlights Lennar's focus amidst market conditions and past financial performance indicated by analyst notes.

feed.title: The career that keeps Southeast Texas moving
feed.summary: This article highlights Scott Nichols, a control room operator at Entergy Texas’ Montgomery County Power Station, and his essential role in providing reliable electricity to Southeast Texas. It discusses his journey into power generation, emphasizing the hands-on, problem-solving nature of the job and the growing demand for skilled operators in the region. Entergy Texas is investing in new generation resources and expanding its workforce to meet the area's increasing population and industrial growth.

feed.title: Sempra Axes Vista Pacifico LNG Plan, Cutting Mexico’s Proposed Export Capacity
feed.summary: Sempra has canceled its Vista Pacifico LNG project in Mexico, reducing the country's potential LNG export capacity by approximately 0.5 Bcf/d. The termination of the development agreement with CFE impacts Mexico's role in the global LNG market and affects the Permian Basin's demand outlook for natural gas. This decision comes amidst ongoing developments and challenges in the international natural gas and LNG sectors.

feed.title: Interpreting Controversial Weapons: Portfolio Construction and Performance Implications
feed.summary: This article discusses the challenges investors face in defining "controversial weapons" for sustainable portfolios, highlighting the lack of a universal definition. It uses Italy's Law 220/2021 as a case study to demonstrate how different interpretations of exclusion criteria can affect portfolio construction and performance, noting a surprisingly limited impact on returns. The piece also anticipates that the rise of lethal autonomous weapon systems will further complicate this debate for defense investments.

feed.title: Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) Given New $235.00 Price Target at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) to $235.00, reiterating a "buy" rating and suggesting a 13.97% upside. This adjustment follows Darden's mixed quarterly results, where EPS slightly missed expectations but revenue surpassed forecasts, and the company issued strong FY2026 guidance. Despite some insider selling, institutional investors hold a significant majority of the stock, reflecting overall confidence in the restaurant operator.

feed.title: The Analyst Verdict: PPL In The Eyes Of 10 Experts
feed.summary: Over the past three months, 10 analysts have provided ratings for PPL (NYSE: PPL), resulting in a mixed outlook with an average 12-month price target of $40.3. The company's recent financial performance shows a solid revenue growth rate of 2.85% as of December 31, 2025, and an impressive net margin of 11.7%, though its ROE of 1.82% is below industry standards. PPL maintains a healthy debt-to-equity ratio of 1.3, indicating lower reliance on debt financing.

feed.title: Scientists create ‘half-Möbius’ molecule, probe it with an IBM quantum computer
feed.summary: IBM and university researchers have announced the creation and characterization of a first-ever half-Möbius molecule (C13Cl2), validating its exotic properties using an IBM quantum computer. This discovery showcases how electronic topology can be engineered and demonstrates the practical application of quantum simulation in understanding complex molecular behavior. The work highlights the potential of quantum-centric supercomputing for molecular discovery and advances science in both chemistry and quantum computing.

feed.title: As Supermicro Joins a Key AI Data Center Alliance, Should You Buy SMCI Stock?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has joined an alliance with SK Telecom and Schneider Electric to develop modular AI data centers, aiming to accelerate deployment and lower costs. This partnership positions Supermicro for significant growth amidst exploding AI infrastructure demand. Despite a recent dip, SMCI's strong Q2 2026 performance, insider buying, and attractive valuation suggest potential upside, with analysts giving it a "Moderate Buy" rating.

feed.title: Amphenol Stock Rises 20% in 6 Months: Is There More Room for Growth?
feed.summary: Amphenol (APH) stock has surged 20.1% in the past six months, outperforming its sector and peers, driven by strong AI-led data center demand, diversified end-market exposure, and strategic acquisitions. The company's diversified portfolio across various sectors like automotive, aerospace, and communications, along with recent acquisitions totaling nearly $2 billion in annualized sales in 2025 and the acquisition of CommScope’s CCS business in 2026, positions it for continued expansion. Despite trading at a premium valuation, analysts are confident in Amphenol's Q1 2026 outlook, with projected revenue growth between $6.90 billion and $7 billion, reinforcing its compelling investment opportunity.

feed.title: FRMI FINAL DEADLINE ALERT: Fermi Inc. Facing Securities Class Action Over IPO And Subsequent Disclosures -- Hagens Berman – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: Hagens Berman has issued an updated notice to investors in Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) concerning a pending securities class action lawsuit. Investors are urged to contact Hagens Berman before the March 6, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline, as the lawsuit alleges that Fermi misrepresented the demand for its "Project Matador" AI data center campus and the stability of its primary anchor tenant, leading to a significant drop in stock price.

feed.title: FirstService (NASDAQ:FSV) Sets New 52-Week Low - What's Next?
feed.summary: FirstService (NASDAQ:FSV) shares recently hit a new 52-week low of $147.13, trading below major moving averages despite generally positive analyst ratings and a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of $211.67. The company reported a modest beat on Q4 earnings, with EPS of $1.37 and revenue of $1.38 billion, and increased its quarterly dividend. Institutional investors have made various adjustments to their holdings, with 69.35% of the stock owned by hedge funds.
